title: FONY Web3 Music Player - Complete Documentation and User Guide
description: FONY is a browser-based music player combining free internet radio streaming with Web3 NFT audio playback, an AI console, and a set of discovery/customization tools. This documentation covers all features, usage tips, chat commands, and technical details for users and developers.

sections.title: Introduction to FONY
sections.content: FONY is a browser-based music player that combines free internet radio streaming with Web3 NFT audio playback, an AI console, and a set of discovery/customization tools — all in a single static web app, with no account or subscription required. This documentation serves as a guide to help you explore all of FONY's functionality.

sections.title: Core Features of FONY
sections.content: FONY's feature set blends internet radio streaming with Web3 audio and a set of discovery tools.
items: Radio Streaming: 24/7 internet radio across dozens of genre-based stations, with live ICY now-playing metadata, automatic stall detection and recovery, global search, shuffle, a Random button, shareable deep links, and session persistence across reloads.

Genres & Playlists: dozens of built-in genre stations, each backed by a plain M3U playlist. Add your own playlist by pasting an M3U URL, or browse a large third-party community M3U catalog directly from the app.

Web3 / NFT Audio: connect a MetaMask-compatible wallet to browse and stream audio from a small, curated allowlist of NFT collections on Ethereum mainnet and Soneum. Metadata (audio, cover art, title, artist) is read from each token's on-chain metadata, with real seeking since NFT tracks have a fixed duration unlike a live stream. This is a curated showcase, not general 'connect any NFT' support, and ownership is not strictly verified for every collection.
AI Console: an in-app chat console with slash-commands for track discovery, facts, mood-based station matching, and more, backed by an external API — see Chat Commands below.

Now Playing & Metadata: Discogs-derived album info (year, country, label, genre) and cover art shown automatically as the track changes, with an animated rotating record/CD/reel visual and Media Session API support for lock-screen and OS-level media controls.

Equalizer & Visualizers: five selectable real-time frequency visualizer styles (bars, line, fish, lava, sheep) driven by the Web Audio API, plus a separate 3-band audio equalizer (low/mid/high) with per-genre presets.

Favorites & Collection: heart any station to pin it as a Favorite, with sorting by custom drag order, date added, genre, or alphabetically. The Collection is a separate, chat-driven list of manually saved tracks used to power AI recommendations.
Theming & Skins: a light/dark theme toggle with a matching icon set, plus procedurally generated background skins layered independently on top of the theme.
Settings & Data Backup: hide non-HTTPS stations, switch to a left-handed layout, and back up, restore, or reset the app's local data — all from the Settings panel.
Fonypedia: a standalone genre encyclopedia with a static reference page for each major genre, browsable independently of the player.

sections.title: Getting Started with FONY
sections.content: To begin, simply select a genre and browse its list of internet radio stations, then use the playback controls to start listening — no account needed. Connect a MetaMask-compatible wallet if you'd like to try Web3 mode and stream from the curated NFT collections. Open the chat console any time for AI-powered discovery commands and a rotating tips walkthrough on first use.

commands.description: Suggests similar tracks to what's currently playing.
Shares quick facts about the current artist or track.
Looks up album/year/label/genre info via Discogs, with cover art and streaming search links.
Describe a mood or vibe (e.g. "90s rock", "something chill") and get matched to a live station currently playing something that fits.
Save the currently playing track to a personal list, and get AI recommendations based on it.
Apply a genre-tuned EQ preset, or toggle an "Analog FX" mode that simulates old-radio warmth.
Generate a random procedural background pattern for the player, or use the current track's album art as a skin.
Shows crypto donation addresses to support FONY's development.

sections.title: Tips for Using FONY
tips: Shuffle and Random: use the shuffle button to cycle through stations in a genre, or hit Random to jump to a random genre and station entirely.
Favorites: heart a station to pin it, then sort your favorites by drag order, date added, genre, or alphabetically.
Custom playlists: paste any M3U URL (including GitHub blob links) to add your own station list, or pick one from the community catalog.
Deep links: share a specific station with `#Genre/<hash>` links, or visit a genre URL directly (e.g. `/techno`) to auto-play it.
Web3 mode: connecting a wallet unlocks a curated set of NFT audio collections — it's a showcase, not a general NFT player.
Settings: back up your local data to a file before clearing browser storage, so favorites and custom playlists aren't lost.

sections.title: Technical Overview
sections.content: FONY is a static, vanilla JavaScript web app — no framework, no bundler, no build step, no package.json. index.html loads ES modules directly (js/main.js as the entry point), which import the rest of the app (player.js, playlist.js, chat.js, web3.js, and more).
items: Hosting: deployed on Netlify as a static site; a `_redirects` file provides SPA-style catch-all routing so deep links like /techno resolve to the app.
Data: genre stations are plain .m3u playlists, indexed by json/playlists.json.
Backend: dynamic functionality (AI chat, stream proxying for mixed-content/iOS HLS relay, Discogs lookups) is handled by an external API service, not included in this repository.
Web3: wallet connectivity and contract reads use ethers.js, loaded from a CDN at runtime.
External services: Discogs (via backend proxy), public IPFS/Arweave gateways, GitHub's API (for the community playlist browser), and Google Analytics for basic usage stats.

sections.title: Running FONY Locally
sections.content: FONY is a static site with no install step. Serve the repository root with any static file server — opening index.html directly via file:// will break, since the app fetches JSON/M3U files at runtime.
items: npx serve .
python -m http.server 8000
netlify dev (since a netlify.toml is present)

sections.title: Known Limitations
items: The AI console, stream proxy, and Discogs lookups depend on an external backend service not included in this repository, so their behavior can't be fully controlled or audited from this codebase alone.
Web3 playback is a curated allowlist of a few NFT collections, not a general-purpose NFT player, and ownership is not strictly enforced for playback on every collection.
A couple of features present in the code (podcast narration, album-art lookup via MusicBrainz) are currently disabled in the UI and not user-reachable.
